mappings.rationaleAI: Extraction of geological materials including metals, coal, gemstones, and industrial minerals corresponds to mining and quarrying (B.1), with surface and underground operations (B.1.a) yielding coal, stone, and metal ores (B.1.b).
Silver production, mostly as a byproduct of copper, gold, lead, and zinc refining, corresponds to production and processing of silver (C.3.m).
Extraction and performance of metallic elements, covering mineral processing, reduction, and phase transformations, corresponds to production and processing of metal ores and metals (C.3).
Wood processing into roundwood, sawn wood, veneer, plywood, and laminated timber corresponds to obtaining wood products from plant materials (C.8.b).
Salt production from salt mines and sea evaporation corresponds to processing of water to obtain salt (C.6).
Hydrogen production via steam reforming and electrolysis corresponds to processing of water to obtain hydrogen (C.6) and processing of air (C.7).
Silicon occurring as silica and silicates, used in glass, ceramics, and cement, corresponds to manufacture of glass (C.5.a) and industrial ceramics (C.5.c).
Iron production via blast furnace, direct reduction, and electric arc processes corresponds to production and processing of iron (C.3.g).
Crude oil recovery by drilling and unconventional means corresponds to petroleum and natural gas production (B.2), and refining by distillation corresponds to petroleum refining (C.1).
Copper smelting and alloying with tin (bronze) and zinc (brass) corresponds to production and processing of copper (C.3.e).
Metals characterized by conductivity, ductility, and malleability, classified as ferrous/non-ferrous, correspond broadly to production and processing of metal ores and metals (C.3).
Iron-carbon alloy production from bloomery furnaces through basic oxygen steelmaking corresponds to steel and steel alloys (C.3.o).
Coal, petroleum, and natural gas as non-renewable energy sources correspond to coal as a mining product (B.1.b) and petroleum/natural gas production (B.2).
Portland cement as the binding agent in concrete corresponds to manufacture of cement and gypsum plasters (C.5.b).
Natural rubber harvested as latex corresponds to obtaining nonfood products from plant and animal processing (C.8.c).
Gold occurring in native form, veins, and alluvial deposits corresponds to production and processing of gold (C.3.f).
Copper-tin alloy corresponds to metal alloys (C.3.v) as a historically significant product of metallurgical processing.
Coal as a mining product (B.1.b) and its utilization for production of coke, coal tar, and chemicals (C.2).
Nitrogen constituting 78% of Earth's atmosphere corresponds to processing of air to obtain nitrogen (C.7).
Oxygen as the most abundant crustal element corresponds to processing of air to obtain oxygen (C.7).
Metallic mixtures combining elements for improved properties correspond to metal alloys (C.3.v).
Naturally occurring solid inorganic substances correspond to the identification and distribution of natural resources (A.1).
Tree-dominated ecosystems as biological resources (A.1), subject to conservation (A.2.a), and the source of wood products (C.8.b).
Cellulosic staple fiber harvested from seed bolls corresponds to obtaining natural fibers from plant products (C.8.f).
Fibre-based materials from natural and synthetic sources correspond to obtaining natural fibers (C.8.f) as raw material inputs.
Explosives used in mining operations for blasting correspond to processes of underground and surface mining (B.1.a).
Earth science including economic geology and petroleum geology underpins the identification and distribution of natural resources (A.1).
